//: PRONUNCIATION LEXICON for the Chatterbox voice server. Maps a canonical SURFACE form (spelled as it appears in the narration script / manuscript) to a respelled SPOKEN form the model pronounces correctly. This file affects ONLY the text SENT to /api/generate (applied after tag-stripping, after the 24-hour clock-time rule); the narration .md and its word-for-word fidelity check are never touched. Matching is WHOLE-WORD (word boundaries, so substrings are never corrupted) and CASE-SENSITIVE (proper nouns). Longer surfaces are tried before shorter ones.
//when-to-add: Add an entry ONLY for a SYSTEMATIC mispronunciation -- a word the model says wrong CONSISTENTLY, every render. Do NOT add a word the model usually says right that merely garbled once: a stochastic one-off bad chunk is fixed by RE-RENDERING that chunk, not by a permanent override (which would over-correct the cases the model already gets right). Example of an entry shape (commented out, inert): "Marisol": "Mah-ree-sole". To activate a correction, add a real "Surface": "Spoken" pair to "entries" using hyphenated phonetic syllables the model says well.
